key_insight: EasyNano uses differentiable optimization through ESMFold2 distograms to rapidly design nanobody CDRs for user-specified epitopes, enabling targeted binding in minutes rather than days.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, workstation-scale workflow for epitope-specific protein design, reducing compute cost and latency for therapeutic nanobody engineering.
method: Optimize CDR residue logits by gradient descent through the ESMFold2 pairwise distance distogram.
Use ESMFold2-Fast as a differentiable oracle with a composite loss that includes an epitope proximity term.
Add a full ESMFold2 CA-coordinate prior to keep the nanobody framework pose stable.
evidence_signal: Across six target-framework pairs, EasyNano improved ipTM by up to 0.559 (0.143 to 0.702) and outperformed random CDR baselines, with Ty1 reaching 5.7 sigma above the random mean.
watch_item: Performance depends on practical choices such as wild-type logit initialization, and multi-seed results show diverse local minima, so replicate runs may be needed.
paper_title: EasyNano: rapid epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design via differentiable distogram optimization with ESMFold2
